title: The Rabies Vaccine Debuted Nearly 150 Years Ago Today
gist: On July 6, 1885, Louis Pasteur first administered his experimental rabies vaccine to 9-year-old Joseph Meister, a landmark echoed by a recent Ontario case of an unvaccinated boy who died after bat exposure.

insights.claim: On July 6, 1885, Louis Pasteur administered his experimental rabies vaccine to nine-year-old Joseph Meister, the first person ever treated, after the boy was bitten 14 times by a rabid dog.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Foundational milestone in vaccine and microbiology history.

insights.claim: A recent Canadian case report describes an 11-year-old boy who died of rabies about five weeks after waking with a bat on his face in northern Ontario in 2024, a death a vaccine could have prevented.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Rabies remains lethal and preventable, underscoring post-exposure prophylaxis.
